perturbation.original_text: The recipient agrees to hold all information provided by Manitoba Housing (“Confidential Information”) in trust and confidence and agrees that it shall be used only for the contemplated purposes, shall not be used for any other purpose or disclosed to any third part.
perturbation.changed_text: The recipient agrees to hold all information provided by Manitoba Housing (“Confidential Information”) in trust and confidence and agrees that it shall be used only for the contemplated purposes.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the phrase 'shall not be used for any other purpose or disclosed to any third party,' the agreement no longer explicitly prohibits the recipient from disclosing the confidential information to third parties or using it for purposes other than those contemplated. This directly contradicts the overall purpose of a confidentiality agreement and makes enforcement against such actions difficult.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS, the recipient agrees to review, examine, inspect or obtain such confidential information only for the purposes described above, and to otherwise hold such information confidential pursuant to the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly prohibited the recipient from using confidential information for any other purpose or disclosing it to third parties. The changed text omits this prohibition, directly conflicting with the contradicted text, which states that the recipient agrees to hold such information confidential. This omission undermines the confidentiality agreement, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: No copies will be made or retained of any written information without the permission of Manitoba Housing.
perturbation.changed_text: Copies may be made or retained of any written information with notification to Manitoba Housing.
perturbation.explanation: The change from prohibiting copies without permission to allowing copies with notification creates an internal contradiction. The altered text undermines the initial intention of strict confidentiality and control over the information, now allowing copies if Manitoba Housing is notified, contradicting the previous restrictive stance. It introduces ambiguity about whether Manitoba Housing has the right to refuse.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Manitoba Housing grants no rights to the confidential informa tion. All confidential information shall remain the sole property of Manitoba Housing.
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibited copies without permission, reinforcing Manitoba Housing's control over confidential information. The changed text allows copies with mere notification, directly contradicting the contradicted text which states Manitoba Housing grants no rights to the confidential information and retains sole property. This creates a strong conflict because the changed text implicitly grants a right to the confidential information (the right to copy it), undermining Manitoba Housing's sole ownership and control.

perturbation.original_text: At the conclusion of any discussions or upon demand by Manitoba Housing, a ll confidential information or written notes taken shall be returned to Manitoba Housing.
perturbation.changed_text: At the conclusion of any discussions, confidential information shall be handled as deemed appropriate by the recipient.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the requirement to return confidential information to allowing the recipient to handle it as they see fit, the agreement now contradicts its fundamental purpose. The initial intent was to ensure Manitoba Housing regained control of the sensitive information, but the revised text permits the recipient to decide its fate, conflicting with the confidential nature of the agreement and undermining Manitoba Housing's ownership.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Manitoba Housing grants no rights to the confidential informa tion. All confidential information shall remain the sole property of Manitoba Housing.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates the return of confidential information to Manitoba Housing. The changed text allows the recipient to handle it as they see fit, directly contradicting the contradicted text, which states that all confidential information shall remain the sole property of Manitoba Housing and that Manitoba Housing grants no rights to it. This change creates a clear conflict regarding the ownership and control of confidential information, falling under the Omissions - In Text Contradiction definition by altering a key obligation and creating a contradiction with the stipulated ownership.

perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS, Manitoba Housing may be furnishing ________________________ (the “Recipient”) certain confidential information relating to __________ _______ (Project No/ Reference) ________ ___________________________________________________________ (Project Title & Address) WHEREAS, the recipient agrees to review, examine, inspect or obtain such confidential information only for the purposes described above, and to otherwise hold such information confidential pursuant to the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS, Manitoba Housing may be furnishing ________________________ (the “Recipient”) certain information relating to __________ _______ (Project No/ Reference) ________ ___________________________________________________________ (Project Title & Address) WHEREAS, the recipient agrees to review, examine, inspect or obtain such information only for general business purposes.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the 'confidential' qualifier and the clause requiring the recipient to hold information confidential, and changing purposes to 'general business purposes', the core principle of the agreement is undermined. This creates a contradiction as the document is titled as a confidentiality and non-disclosure agreement, however, this section no longer imposes any confidentiality obligations. The changed text contradicts the very essence of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: BE IT KNOWN , that Manitoba Housing has or may furnish to the Recipient certain confidential information on the following conditions:
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the 'confidential' qualifier and the obligation to hold information confidential, altering the purpose to 'general business purposes.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that Manitoba Housing may furnish 'certain confidential information' under specific conditions. The change fundamentally undermines the confidentiality aspect referenced elsewhere, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement and its validity, construction and effect shall be governed by the laws of Manitoba.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement and its validity shall be interpreted according to principles of fairness.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing the governing law clause with a vague 'principles of fairness' standard, the agreement now lacks legal clarity and is internally contradictory. The removal of a defined legal jurisdiction introduces uncertainty. If there's a dispute, there is no legal system defined to solve it and 'principles of fairness' is incredibly vague. It contradicts the need for legal enforceability implied in any contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS, the recipient agrees to review, examine, inspect or obtain such confidential information only for the purposes described above, and to otherwise hold such information confidential pursuant to the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified Manitoba law as the governing law. The changed text replaces this with 'principles of fairness,' creating a direct conflict. The contradicted text discusses holding information confidential pursuant to the terms of the Agreement, which requires a defined legal framework for enforcement. Replacing the specific governing law with a vague principle undermines the enforceability of the confidentiality terms and the agreement as a whole. Therefore, an omission has occurred that causes an in-text contradiction.
